title: Front-half chapter and filing phase
summary: After the beach outing and front-half transition route land, lane 4 should turn those pieces into a stronger early-game chapter. This wave spreads the front-half routes into a clearer coast-to-forest chapter arc, then deepens the filing payoff so returning to the station feels more like finishing a field page than just clearing a task.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the front-half route-title chapter spread handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The front-half route names are live now, but the board and guided flow still use generic labels that weaken place memory.

execution_order.goal: Implement the front-half route-title chapter spread.
execution_order.why_now: Reusing the real route titles across the current season surfaces should make the early chapter feel more authored without adding another shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the front-half route-title chapter spread.
execution_order.why_now: The chapter spread should strengthen place memory without slipping into a heavier quest or chapter-index feel.

execution_order.goal: Fix the front-half board summary progression.
execution_order.why_now: The route-title spread now advances the active beat title, but the board summary and direction line can still stall on Hidden Hollow after later forest substeps become active.

execution_order.goal: Review the front-half board summary progression fix.
execution_order.why_now: The repair should keep the route-title chapter truthful without adding more shell text or new mechanics.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the Open To Shelter filing-return handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Now that the chapter arc is truthful, the weakest remaining front-half seam is the filed coastal return still sounding like the just-completed transect instead of a page turn into Edge Moisture.

execution_order.goal: Deepen the Open To Shelter filing return.
execution_order.why_now: A compact logged close plus Edge Moisture handoff can make the front-half filing feel more like finishing a field page without adding another shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the Open To Shelter filing return.
execution_order.why_now: The new filing beat should deepen notebook feeling, not add clutter or point back at the already-filed transect.

guardrails: Reuse current Route v2, support, and notebook-return seams.
Do not add another support row, quest log, or station page.
Prefer the existing route titles over inventing a new chapter label family.
Prefer stronger place memory and chapter wording over heavier mechanics.
Keep the front-half chapter calm, readable, and early-game friendly.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-lane-4-front-half-chapter-follow-on.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-front-half-route-title-chapter-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-front-half-route-title-chapter-review.md, docs/reports/2026-04-03-open-to-shelter-filing-depth-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat the next wave as a compact filing-depth pass: once `Open To Shelter` is filed, the routes return should feel like a page turn into `Edge Moisture` rather than pointing back at the already-filed transect, and `note-tabs` may carry one short `OPEN TO SHELTER LOGGED` close in that exact state.
